uploadAlert: Please upload a snake image first
uploadSubtitle: Upload a clear snake image for instant AI identification
dropzoneSubtitle: or click to browse — JPG, PNG supported
disclaimer: AI-based identification. Always consult a wildlife expert for confirmation.

cobra.name: Cobra
cobra.advice: Keep distance immediately. Do not touch the snake. Seek urgent medical help if bitten.
cobra.firstAidNote: This is a highly venomous snake. Immediate medical treatment is critical.

firstAid.title: Stay Calm & Reassure
firstAid.desc: Keep the victim calm and still to slow down any stress response.

firstAid.title: Immobilize the Area
firstAid.desc: Keep the bitten limb still and below heart level.

firstAid.title: Remove Tight Items
firstAid.desc: Remove rings, watches, or tight clothing near the bite.

firstAid.title: Seek Emergency Help
firstAid.desc: Call emergency services or rush to the nearest hospital immediately.

biteRisk.text: Cobra venom is highly neurotoxic and can cause paralysis.
Symptoms include swelling, difficulty breathing, and blurred vision.
Do not attempt to handle or capture this snake. Seek emergency help immediately.
Anti-venom is available — get to a hospital as fast as possible.

hump_nosed_pit_viper.name: Hump Nosed Pit Viper
hump_nosed_pit_viper.advice: Venomous snake. Avoid handling and get medical support immediately if bitten.
hump_nosed_pit_viper.firstAidNote: Hemotoxic venom can cause tissue damage. Medical treatment is essential.

firstAid.title: Stay Calm & Reassure
firstAid.desc: Keep the victim calm and still to slow down any stress response.

firstAid.title: Clean the Wound
firstAid.desc: Gently wash the bite area with soap and clean water.

firstAid.title: Monitor Symptoms
firstAid.desc: Watch for pain, swelling, or other unusual symptoms.

firstAid.title: Seek Medical Help
firstAid.desc: Get medical attention immediately even if symptoms seem mild.

biteRisk.text: Hump-nosed pit viper venom causes local tissue destruction.
Symptoms include severe pain, swelling, and bleeding disorders.
Do not attempt to handle or capture this snake. Maintain a safe distance.
Seek medical attention promptly even if the bite seems minor.

indian_rock_python.name: Indian Rock Python
indian_rock_python.advice: Non-venomous, but keep distance and contact wildlife rescue.
indian_rock_python.firstAidNote: This snake is non-venomous. Serious medical treatment is usually not required.

firstAid.title: Stay Calm & Reassure
firstAid.desc: Keep the victim calm and still to slow down any stress response.

firstAid.title: Clean the Wound
firstAid.desc: Gently wash the bite area with soap and clean water.

firstAid.title: Monitor Symptoms
firstAid.desc: Watch for pain, swelling, or other unusual symptoms.

firstAid.title: Seek Medical Help if Needed
firstAid.desc: Get medical attention if symptoms worsen or persist.

biteRisk.text: Indian Rock Python is non-venomous and poses no danger to humans.
However, it may bite if provoked or handled roughly.
Do not attempt to handle or capture this snake. Maintain a safe distance and let it move away.
If bitten, clean the wound and consult a doctor if you experience any discomfort.

krait.name: Krait
krait.behavior: Docile by day, Aggressive at night
krait.advice: Highly venomous snake. Get emergency medical attention if bitten.
krait.firstAidNote: Krait bites are often painless but extremely dangerous. Seek help immediately.

firstAid.title: Stay Calm & Reassure
firstAid.desc: Keep the victim calm and still to slow down any stress response.

firstAid.title: Immobilize the Area
firstAid.desc: Keep the bitten limb still and below heart level.

firstAid.title: Do Not Cut or Suck
firstAid.desc: Never cut the wound or attempt to suck out venom.

firstAid.title: Seek Emergency Help
firstAid.desc: Call emergency services or rush to the nearest hospital immediately.

biteRisk.text: Krait venom is highly neurotoxic — bites are often painless but deadly.
Symptoms include muscle paralysis, difficulty breathing, and drooping eyelids.
Do not attempt to handle or capture this snake. Seek emergency help immediately.
Anti-venom is available — time is critical, get to a hospital immediately.

viper.name: Viper
viper.advice: Venomous snake. Stay away and seek medical help if bitten.
viper.firstAidNote: Russell's Viper is one of the most dangerous snakes. Immediate treatment is critical.

firstAid.title: Stay Calm & Reassure
firstAid.desc: Keep the victim calm and still to slow down any stress response.

firstAid.title: Immobilize the Area
firstAid.desc: Keep the bitten limb still and below heart level.

firstAid.title: Remove Tight Items
firstAid.desc: Remove rings, watches, or tight clothing near the bite.

firstAid.title: Seek Emergency Help
firstAid.desc: Call emergency services or rush to the nearest hospital immediately.

biteRisk.text: Russell's Viper venom causes severe internal bleeding and organ failure.
Symptoms include intense pain, swelling, bleeding gums, and kidney failure.
Do not attempt to handle or capture this snake. Seek emergency help immediately.
Anti-venom is available — get to a hospital as fast as possible.
